macro_segments.title: The Identity of "Him" in the Mishnah: Husband vs. Adulterer and the Parallel Language of Checking

micro_segments.title: Opening Question: Who is the "Him" Being Checked—Husband or Adulterer?
Establishing the Basic Rule: Waters Check Both Her and the Adulterer Only If He Is Free of Sexual Sin
The Parallel Language Resolution: Otah/Otoh and Ba'al/Bo'el Explain the Mishnah's Phrasing
Yerushalmi Source: The Waters' Impact on Multiple Forbidden Acts and the Gematria of Marerim

macro_segments.title: Consequences of Her Forbidden Status: Restrictions on the Adulterer's Family and Her Own Marriage

micro_segments.title: She Cannot Marry the Adulterer's Brother, Even in Cases of Yibum
The Status Problem: How Her Forbidden Status Works Through Her, Not the Man

macro_segments.title: Analyzing Asymmetrical Outcomes: When Only One Party Is Affected by the Waters

micro_segments.title: Scenario One: Waters Affect Him But Not Her—Merit as an Explanation
Resolving the First Scenario: She Must Be Innocent; He Must Be Intentional While She Was Unwitting
Scenario Two: Waters Affect Her But Not Him—What Can Be Concluded?
Resolving the Second Scenario: He Was Unwitting; Can She Now Marry Him If Divorced?
Summary of All Four Cases: Combinations of Intentionality and Their Marital Consequences

macro_segments.title: Deriving the Source for "Him" from the Verses: Rabbi Akiva vs. Rabbi Yishmael on the Extra Vav

micro_segments.title: The Dispute: Does the Drasha Come from the Extra Vav or from Word Repetition?
Rabbi Akiva's Approach: Six Instances (Three Vavim and Three Baus) and Their Meanings
The Three Meanings: Tzava (Command), Asiyah (Action), and Yedia (Informing)
Rabbi Yishmael's Approach: Three Instances Without the Vavim; Resolving the Three Baus

macro_segments.title: The Three-Fold Consequence of Tumah (Impurity): To Husband, Adulterer, and Terumah

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Akiva's View: Three Impacts from the Three Mentions of Nitme'ah
Rabbi Yishmael's Kal Vachomer: Fourth Consequence—Prohibition to Marry a Kohen

macro_segments.title: The Sotah's Status by Doubt: Why Repetition of Nitme'ah Teaches Presumed Guilt Until Proven Innocent

micro_segments.title: The Question: Why Does the Torah Repeat Nitme'ah If Her Status Is Uncertain?
The Answer: She Is Definitely Forbidden by Virtue of the Doubt Alone
Tosafot's Analysis: The Status Is an Issur Asei Even If She Is Innocent

macro_segments.title: Learning Laws of Ritual Purity from the Sotah: Safek Tumah in a Private Domain

micro_segments.title: The Comparison: Sotah's Doubt Status Applies to Sheretz and Other Tumah Cases
Key Definition: Reshut Hayachid (Private Domain) as the Locus of the Rule
The Role of Kinui and Sitrah: Creating Safek Rather Than Establishing Likelihood
The Requirement of Yesh Bo Da'at Lisha'el: Why Someone Must Be Able to Know
Final Rule: Safek Tumah Is Tamei in Private Domain with Potential Witnesses; Tahor Otherwise
